scenario_results.reason: Completion progression required evidence, readiness gates, and routing clarity were validated before case disposition.
Missing artifacts and approval context correctly routed to exception handling instead of progression.
Completion refusal was triggered by missing release and quality evidence, and a deficiency record was returned.
Boundary-safe refusal was returned for legal/award/policy-authority requests with adjacent owner routing.
Maintenance readiness conflict was escalated as an execution exception and placed in rework-oriented exception queue.
Defense terminology was normalized and tied to evidence and artifact gates before progression.
Missing tenant policy and approval matrix created low-confidence escalation with explicit assumption checklist.
Public versus tenant policy conflict was surfaced as a source-conflict escalation without overconfident disposition.
ERP, MES, and supplier conflict was mapped and held with traceability conflict escalation rather than unsafe closeout.
Supplier substitution beyond authority was declined and routed to supplier-quality and contract approvers.
Active hold and nonconformance context preserved the release boundary and blocked completion.
Approval-owner ambiguity was resolved as a routing blocker, with explicit owner-resolution escalation before completion.

review_notes: Functionality coverage includes all shared, workflow_phase_group, and industry_profile abilities and maps each required scenario ID.

notes: Scenario outcomes intentionally enforce evidence-backed completion and boundary refusal.
